**lebop v0.0.3** — agent context + reviewed publish release.

This release turns lebop from a command collection into a fuller
agent-facing Linear product surface. The major theme is fewer calls for
research, safer mutation paths, and a release gate that proves CLI and MCP
behavior against the Noxor sandbox workspace.

## Highlights

- **Workspace context tools for agents.** `lebop workspace explore` /
  `explore_linear_workspace` provide ls-style Linear discovery, while
  `lebop workspace fetch` / `fetch_linear_workspace` materialize bounded
  local dossiers for projects, issues, initiatives, documents, cycles,
  milestones, and agent sessions, including issue-scoped document child
  paths such as `/issues/TEAM-123/documents`.
- **Reviewed publish workflow.** `publish review` and `publish apply`
  give agents a review-before-mutation path for plans and cache edits,
  with local hash checks, remote `updatedAt` checks, and post-apply
  verification.
- **CLI/MCP parity.** The public surfaces now share the same library core
  and are covered by a source-controlled tool manifest plus contract tests.
- **Truthful mutation reporting.** Several write paths now distinguish
  remote mutation success from local cache writeback failure instead of
  collapsing both into a generic success.
- **Live Noxor validation.** The full live harness exercises the CLI and
  MCP surfaces against the sandbox workspace and requires complete
  surface, semantic, and cleanup coverage before release.

## Agent-facing changes

- Added concise workspace exploration for root, collections, concrete
  entities, and search paths.
- Added local context dossier materialization with manifests,
  continuations, and bounded pagination semantics.
- Added higher-level publish tools: `review_linear_changes` and
  `publish_linear_changes`.
- Added MCP `update_issue` label deltas with `labels_add` /
  `labels_remove`, plus MCP `update_relations` for one-call relation
  add/remove batches.
- Split MCP auth identity into read-only `whoami` and explicit
  `refresh_whoami`.
- Added MCP `lint_files` for CLI-equivalent path/cache linting, while
  `lint_text fix:true` remains the in-memory content linter. File lint
  fix mode now reports remaining post-fix warnings and counts instead of
  stale warnings that were already rewritten.
- Live release reports now include and validate workspace/team/stamp plus
  compiled-binary provenance: version, SHA-256, byte size, platform, and
  architecture.
- Added behavior-contract validation for context and publish payloads.
- Expanded the bundled agent skill to prefer explore/fetch for broad
  Linear research and publish review/apply for state-changing work.

## Correctness and safety

- Relation lookup/diff/pull paths now page through relation connections
  instead of trusting a single Linear page.
- Direct plan apply enforces the `updatedAt` stale guard for relation-only changes.
- Comment creation rejects empty bodies and treats a missing returned
  comment entity as an error.
- Label name resolution now has explicit team vs workspace scope semantics
  across CLI and MCP.
- Destructive and mutation-capable MCP operations require deterministic
  confirmation arguments; `bulk_update_issues` now supports `dry_run` preview
  and requires `confirm: true` for real mutations.
- Auth list/whoami JSON uses the logical `LEBOP_HOME/auth.json` location
  instead of exposing absolute local auth paths.

## Release validation

- Package/runtime version metadata is sourced from `package.json`.
- Release builds still compile four binaries:
  `lebop-{darwin,linux}-{x64,arm64}`.
- The Linux x64 compiled binary is credentialed against Noxor in the
  release workflow before publishing.
- `LEBOP_LIVE_BIN=/path/to/lebop` lets the live harness run against a
  compiled binary instead of the source wrapper.
- The published installer smoke now exercises real command-family help and
  an installed MCP `initialize` handshake before promoting a release.

## Install

```sh
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/N0xMare/lebop/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```

macOS / Linux, x64 / arm64. Verifies SHA256 against the `SHA256SUMS`
attached to this release. Pin this release with `LEBOP_VERSION=v0.0.3`.

## Upgrade notes

- This is the next patch release after `v0.0.2` and is the recommended
  baseline for agent-driven Linear workflows.
- Agents should prefer `workspace explore` / `workspace fetch` for broad
  research and `publish review` / `publish apply` for reviewed mutations.
- Scripts that consume JSON should continue using `--json`; wrapped
  commands use the stable `{schema_version: 1, ...}` envelope.
